# CLI Wrappers - MCP Tool Collection

> Token-efficient CLI wrappers for Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers
> Location: `~/cli-wrappers/`
> Status: Production-ready
> Total Tools: 17 wrappers with 4-level progressive disclosure
> Token Efficiency: 60-70% savings vs traditional MCP tool calling

---

## Overview

CLI Wrappers is a collection of self-contained Python CLI tools that wrap MCP servers. Every tool implements a consistent 4-level progressive disclosure pattern to minimize token usage while maintaining complete functionality.

### Key Characteristics

- Progressive Disclosure: Level 1 (~30 tokens) through Level 4 (~500 tokens)
- No Installation: PEP 723 inline dependencies with `uv` package manager
- Consistent Interface: All tools follow the same CLI patterns
- Multiple Output Formats: JSON, text, table
- Production-Ready: Error handling, validation, timeout protection

---

## Architecture

- Language: Python 3.10+
- Package Manager: uv (Astral) with PEP 723 script execution
- CLI Framework: Click (hand-crafted), argparse (generated)
- HTTP Client: httpx with timeout protection
- MCP Client: fastmcp >= 2.0
- Output: Rich library for terminal formatting

### Wrapper Types

1. Auto-Generated (template-based): Use `templates/wrapper.jinja2`, argparse CLI, fastmcp tool calling
2. Hand-Crafted (feature-rich): Click CLI, Rich output, full API integration, located in `*-cli/` subdirectories

---

## 4-Level Progressive Disclosure

```
Level 1 (--help):        Quick overview               ~30 tokens
Level 2 (list/info):     Function catalog & docs      ~150 tokens
Level 3 (example):       Working code examples        ~200 tokens
Level 4 (FUNC --help):   Complete reference           ~500 tokens
```

```bash
uv run crawl4ai.py --help          # Level 1
uv run crawl4ai.py list            # Level 2
uv run crawl4ai.py info scrape     # Level 2
uv run crawl4ai.py example scrape  # Level 3
uv run crawl4ai.py scrape --help   # Level 4
```

---

## Complete Tool Catalog

### Web Scraping & Content Extraction

#### crawl4ai.py
- Purpose: Fast web scraping and content extraction
- API Key: None (local MCP server)
- Functions:
  - `scrape(url)` - Scrape single webpage
  - `crawl(url, depth, max_pages)` - Crawl multiple pages
  - `extract_content(url, selector)` - CSS selector extraction
- Detail Levels: minimal, standard, full
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run crawl4ai.py scrape --url https://example.com
  uv run crawl4ai.py crawl --url https://docs.python.org --crawl-depth 2 --max-pages 10
  uv run crawl4ai.py scrape --url https://example.com --detail minimal --format json
  ```

#### firecrawl.py
- Purpose: Advanced web scraping with JavaScript rendering
- API Key: `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY` (required)
- MCP Package: `@anthropics/firecrawl-mcp`
- Functions:
  - `scrape(url, formats, only-main-content, wait-for)` - Scrape with JS rendering
  - `crawl(url, limit, max-depth, include-paths, exclude-paths)` - Recursive crawling
  - `extract(url, schema, prompt)` - Structured data extraction with LLM
  - `map(url, search, limit)` - Discover all URLs on a site
  - `search(query, limit, lang, country)` - Web search
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run firecrawl.py scrape --url "https://spa-app.com"
  uv run firecrawl.py crawl --url "https://docs.example.com" --limit 50
  uv run firecrawl.py map --url "https://example.com"
  uv run firecrawl.py extract --url "https://store.com/product" --schema '{"name":"string","price":"number"}'
  uv run firecrawl.py search --query "machine learning tutorials"
  ```

#### gitmcp.py
- Purpose: Fetch documentation from GitHub repositories
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `fetch_generic_documentation(owner, repo)` - Get llms.txt/README
  - `search_generic_documentation(owner, repo, query)` - Search repo docs
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run gitmcp.py fetch_generic_documentation --owner anthropics --repo claude-code
  uv run gitmcp.py search_generic_documentation --owner jlowin --repo fastmcp --query "tools"
  ```

---

### Search & Information Retrieval

#### exa.py
- Purpose: Neural semantic web search and agentic research
- API Key: `EXA_API_KEY` (required)
- Version: 1.1.0
- Functions:
  - `search(query, num_results, type, category, include_domains, exclude_domains, start_published_date, include_text, include_highlights, include_summary)` - Semantic neural search
  - `deep-search(query, additional_queries, num_results, include_text, include_summary)` - Comprehensive search with query expansion
  - `research(instructions, model, output_schema)` - Agentic research: plans, searches, and synthesizes across sources with citations
  - `code-search(query, num_results, include_domains, include_text, include_highlights)` - Find code on GitHub, Stack Overflow, and docs
  - `company-research(company_name, num_results, include_news, start_date, include_summary)` - Business intelligence
  - `find-similar(url, num_results, include_text)` - Semantic similarity content discovery
  - `get-contents(urls, text_verbosity, include_html_tags, livecrawl, max_characters)` - Extract full content from URLs
- Search Types: auto, neural, fast
- Research Models: exa-research (45-90s), exa-research-pro (90-180s, max quality)
- Categories: company, research_paper, news, tweet, personal_site, financial_report, people
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run exa.py search "machine learning transformers 2025"
  uv run exa.py research "What are the tradeoffs between gRPC and REST?"
  uv run exa.py code-search "async await Python examples"
  uv run exa.py get-contents "https://example.com/article" --livecrawl
  ```

#### ref-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Technical documentation search and reading
- API Key: `REF_API_KEY` (required)
- Functions:
  - `search(query, limit, filter)` - Search technical documentation
  - `read_url(url, extract_code, include_metadata)` - Extract doc content
  - `check(filepath, strict)` - Validate files
  - `analyze(path, recursive, include, exclude)` - Analyze directory
  - `config()` - Show configuration
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run ref-cli/cli.py search --query "async await python"
  uv run ref-cli/cli.py read_url --url "https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html"
  ```

#### brave-search-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Privacy-focused web search
- API Key: `BRAVE_API_KEY` (required)
- Functions:
  - `web_search(query, country, count, safesearch, freshness)` - Web search
  - `local_search(query, country, count)` - Local business search (Pro)
  - `video_search(query)` - Video search
  - `image_search(query)` - Image search
  - `news_search(query, freshness)` - News search
  - `summarizer(query)` - AI summaries
- Freshness: pd (past day), pw (past week), pm (past month)
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run brave-search-cli/cli.py web_search "python tutorials"
  uv run brave-search-cli/cli.py news_search "AI developments" --freshness pw
  ```

#### tavily-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: AI-optimized web search
- API Key: `TAVILY_API_KEY` (required)
- Functions:
  - `search(query, search_depth, include_answer, include_sources)` - Optimized search
  - `search_context(query, topic, context)` - Search with context
  - `qna(query)` - Question & answer search
- Search Depths: basic, advanced
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run tavily-cli/cli.py search --query "React hooks best practices"
  uv run tavily-cli/cli.py qna --query "What is the JAM stack?"
  ```

#### perplexity-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Perplexity AI search integration
- API Key: `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY` (required)
- Functions:
  - `search(query, model, focus)` - Perplexity search
  - `stream(query)` - Streaming search results
  - `research(topic, num_sources)` - Deep research mode
- Models: pplx-7b-online, pplx-70b-online
- Focus: internet, academic, writing
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run perplexity-cli/cli.py search --query "latest AI developments"
  uv run perplexity-cli/cli.py research --topic "quantum computing"
  ```

---

### Code & Repository Analysis

#### deepwiki.py
- Purpose: Ask questions about any GitHub repository
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `ask(owner, repo, question)` - Ask about a repository
  - `explain(owner, repo, path)` - Explain a file or directory
  - `architecture(owner, repo)` - Get architectural overview
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run deepwiki.py ask --owner facebook --repo react --question "How does the reconciler work?"
  uv run deepwiki.py architecture --owner apple --repo swift
  ```

#### repomix.py
- Purpose: Pack codebases for AI analysis
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `pack_codebase(path, include, exclude, output)` - Pack local codebase
  - `pack_remote_repository(owner, repo, output)` - Pack GitHub repo
  - `generate_skill(path, output)` - Generate Claude skill
  - `attach_packed_output(data, filepath)` - Attach packed output
  - `grep_repomix_output(content, pattern)` - Search packed content
- Filters: `--include "*.swift"`, `--exclude "Tests/*"`
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run repomix.py pack_codebase ~/my-project
  uv run repomix.py pack_remote_repository --owner jlowin --repo fastmcp
  ```

#### semly.py
- Purpose: Semantic code search and analysis
- API Key: `SEMLY_API_KEY` (required)
- Functions:
  - `outline(path, recursive)` - Generate code outline
  - `search(query, path, language)` - Semantic code search
  - `analyze(filepath)` - Analyze file structure
  - `references(symbol, path)` - Find symbol references
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run semly.py outline /path/to/project
  uv run semly.py search "authentication" --path /path/to/codebase
  ```

---

### Reasoning & Orchestration

#### sequential-thinking-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Multi-step reasoning chains for complex problems
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `sequentialthinking(thought, thoughtNumber, totalThoughts, nextThoughtNeeded)` - Chain reasoning
- Parameters: thought (string), thoughtNumber (int), totalThoughts (int), nextThoughtNeeded (bool), isRevision (bool), revisesThought (int), branchFromThought (int), branchId (string)
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run sequential-thinking-cli/cli.py sequentialthinking \
    --thought "Analyzing the root cause" --thoughtNumber 1 \
    --totalThoughts 5 --nextThoughtNeeded true
  ```

#### claude-flow.py
- Purpose: Multi-agent orchestration and workflow coordination
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `create_swarm(agents, config)` - Create agent swarm
  - `execute_workflow(config)` - Execute multi-step workflow
  - `coordinate_agents(tasks, dependencies)` - Coordinate agent tasks
  - `list()` - List active swarms
  - `status(swarm_id)` - Get swarm status
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run claude-flow.py create_swarm --agents "researcher,implementer,reviewer"
  uv run claude-flow.py execute_workflow --config workflow.yaml
  ```

#### flow-nexus.py
- Purpose: Sandboxed code execution environments
- API Key: None
- Functions:
  - `execute(code, language, timeout)` - Execute code in sandbox
  - `run_script(file, sandbox, language)` - Run script in isolation
  - `list_environments()` - List available environments
  - `create_environment(python_version, packages)` - Create custom environment
- Supported Languages: Python, JavaScript, Go, Rust, Ruby
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run flow-nexus.py execute --code "print('hello')" --language python
  uv run flow-nexus.py run_script --file script.py --sandbox true
  ```

---

### Development Tools

#### xcode-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Xcode build, test, and project control
- API Key: None (requires macOS with Xcode)
- Functions:
  - Project: `xcode_open_project()`, `xcode_close_project()`, `xcode_refresh_project()`
  - Info: `xcode_get_schemes()`, `xcode_get_workspace_info()`, `xcode_open_file()`
  - Build: `xcode_build()`, `xcode_clean()`, `xcode_build_and_run()`
  - Test: `xcode_test()` with test plan and selection
  - Debug: `xcode_debug()`, `xcode_stop()`
  - Analysis: `xcresult_summary()`, `xcresult_test_details()`, `xcresult_screenshots()`, `xcresult_logs()`
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run xcode-cli/cli.py xcode_get_schemes --xcodeproj /path/to/MyApp.xcodeproj
  uv run xcode-cli/cli.py xcode_build --xcodeproj /path/to/MyApp.xcodeproj --scheme MyApp
  uv run xcode-cli/cli.py xcode_test --xcodeproj /path/to/MyApp.xcodeproj --destination "iPhone 15"
  uv run xcode-cli/cli.py xcresult_summary --xcresult_path /path/to/Test.xcresult
  ```

#### apple-docset-cli/cli.py
- Purpose: Query local Apple/Xcode documentation
- API Key: None (requires macOS with Xcode)
- Functions:
  - `list_docsets()` - List available DocSets
  - `search(query, limit, docset)` - Search API symbols
  - `fetch(docset, path)` - Get full documentation
  - `export(docset, output, max)` - Export to JSONL
  - `availability(symbol, docset)` - Check API availability
- Usage:
  ```bash
  uv run apple-docset-cli/cli.py list_docsets
  uv run apple-docset-cli/cli.py search "NSView" --limit 10
  uv run apple-docset-cli/cli.py fetch --docset "Apple API Reference" --path "documentation/appkit/nsview"
  ```

---

## Response Formats

### Standard Response
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "data": {},
    "count": 5,
    "timestamp": "2025-02-22T10:30:00Z"
}
```

### Error Response
```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": "Description",
    "error_code": "INVALID_ARGUMENT",
    "tool": "tool_name",
    "recovery": "Suggested fix"
}
```

### Output Format Flags
```bash
--format json       # Machine-readable (default)
--format text       # Human-readable
--format table      # ASCII tables
--detail minimal    # Status only (~50 tokens)
--detail standard   # Preview (~150 tokens)
--detail full       # Complete (default)
```

---

## Environment Variables

### Required API Keys

| Variable | Tool | Required |
|----------|------|----------|
| `EXA_API_KEY` | exa.py | Yes |
| `REF_API_KEY` | ref-cli | Yes |
| `BRAVE_API_KEY` | brave-search-cli | Yes |
| `TAVILY_API_KEY` | tavily-cli | Yes |
| `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY` | perplexity-cli | Yes |
| `SEMLY_API_KEY` | semly.py | Yes |

### Optional

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `CRAWL4AI_MCP_DIR` | Local MCP server path | Auto-detect |
| `EXA_API_URL` | Exa API endpoint | https://api.exa.ai |
| `EXA_TIMEOUT` | Exa request timeout in seconds | 60 |
| `TIMEOUT` | Global timeout | 30s |
| `VERBOSE` | Enable verbose output | False |

---

## Project Structure

```
cli-wrappers/
+-- CLAUDE.md                     # Development rules (authoritative)
+-- README.md                     # User-facing overview
+-- ONBOARDING.md                 # Developer guide
+-- QUICKSTART.md                 # 5-minute setup
+-- LLMs.txt                      # This file
+-- LLMs-SUMMARY.txt              # Condensed version
|
+-- # Root-level wrappers (PEP 723 scripts)
+-- crawl4ai.py                   # Web scraping
+-- exa.py                        # Neural semantic search and agentic research (v1.1.0)
+-- deepwiki.py                   # GitHub repo Q&A
+-- gitmcp.py                     # GitHub docs fetching
+-- repomix.py                    # Codebase packing
+-- semly.py                      # Code analysis
+-- claude-flow.py                # Multi-agent orchestration
+-- firecrawl.py                  # Advanced JS scraping
+-- flow-nexus.py                 # Sandboxed execution
|
+-- # Sub-project wrappers (with tests)
+-- brave-search-cli/             # Brave Search
+-- perplexity-cli/               # Perplexity search
+-- ref-cli/                      # Documentation search
+-- sequential-thinking-cli/      # Multi-step reasoning
+-- tavily-cli/                   # Tavily search
+-- xcode-cli/                    # Xcode control
+-- apple-docset-cli/             # Apple documentation
|
+-- # Code generation
+-- generate-all-wrappers.py      # Master generator
+-- templates/
|   +-- wrapper.jinja2            # Wrapper template
|
+-- .claude/                      # Claude Code configuration
+-- .planning/                    # Architecture documentation
```

---

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Discovery
uv run TOOL.py --help
uv run TOOL.py list
uv run TOOL.py info FUNCTION
uv run TOOL.py example FUNCTION
uv run TOOL.py FUNCTION --help

# Web Scraping
uv run crawl4ai.py scrape --url URL

# Search
uv run exa.py search "query"
uv run exa.py research "complex question requiring synthesis"
uv run exa.py code-search "implementation example"
uv run ref-cli/cli.py search --query "docs"
uv run brave-search-cli/cli.py web_search "query"

# Code Analysis
uv run repomix.py pack_codebase PATH
uv run deepwiki.py ask --owner OWNER --repo REPO --question "?"
uv run semly.py outline PATH

# Development
uv run xcode-cli/cli.py xcode_build --xcodeproj PATH --scheme SCHEME
uv run apple-docset-cli/cli.py search "NSView"

# Reasoning
uv run sequential-thinking-cli/cli.py sequentialthinking --thought "..." --thoughtNumber 1 --totalThoughts 5 --nextThoughtNeeded true
```

---

## Getting Started

```bash
cd ~/cli-wrappers
uv run crawl4ai.py --help           # Test installation
uv run crawl4ai.py list             # See functions
uv run crawl4ai.py info scrape      # Function details
uv run crawl4ai.py example scrape   # Working example
uv run crawl4ai.py scrape --url https://example.com  # Execute
```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| `uv: command not found` | `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh \| sh` |
| Missing API key | `export KEY="value"` |
| MCP server not found | `export CRAWL4AI_MCP_DIR=~/path` |
| Connection timeout | `export TIMEOUT=60` |
| Invalid JSON | `uv run tool --format json list \| jq .` |

---

Last Updated: 2026-03-11
Python: 3.10+
Total Tools: 17 wrappers (9 root-level, 7 sub-projects, 1 generator)
Status: Production-ready
Token Efficiency: 60-70% savings
